column1: # Ingress SNI and Host Header Experiment — Raw Results
# Certificate Issuer: Microsoft Azure RSA TLS Issuing CA 03

column8: Baseline: correct SNI + correct Host → routes to intended app
SNI=alpha but Host=beta → Envoy routes by HOST HEADER not SNI. Response is app-beta.
SNI=nonexistent within env wildcard + Host=alpha → TLS succeeds (wildcard cert), routes by Host to alpha
No SNI → Envoy rejects TLS handshake immediately (connection reset by peer). SNI is REQUIRED.
Correct SNI + empty Host → TLS succeeds but Envoy returns 404 (Container App Unavailable page)
SNI=beta + Host=alpha → Routes to alpha. Confirms Host header is the routing key, not SNI.
Valid SNI + unknown Host → TLS succeeds but Envoy returns 404 (no matching app for Host)
SNI outside env wildcard → Connection reset. Envoy only accepts SNI matching environment wildcard.
